response: The descendants of the group of black Indians associated with the Seminole people settled in El Nacimiento, Coahuila, Mexico, and in the area of Hyderabad, India, where they migrated from Uttar Pradesh during the Nizam rule. They also settled in the United States, particularly in Oklahoma and Florida, where they formed distinct bands and were descendants of free blacks and escaped slaves (maroons) who allied with Seminole groups in Spanish Florida.
